name: GT-opnorm
source: Kitaev arXiv:2405.02434, refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:638-642 (operator norm on linear operators; bounded operators form a Banach algebra). VERBATIM: "Since $\calA$ is a Banach space, linear operators on it have a standard norm," ... "\|w\|=\sup_{\|Z\|\le 1}\|w(Z)\|,\qquad w\colon \calA\to\calA," ... "and bounded linear operators form a Banach algebra." This fixes the OPERATOR NORM ||w||=sup_{||Z||<=1}||w(Z)|| of a bounded linear operator (and that bounded operators form a Banach algebra). DERIVED CONSEQUENCE used here (the submultiplicative/composition bound): for bounded linear maps T (acting on the A-side, ||T||_op its operator norm) and j: B->A bounded linear (||j||_op=sup_{||y||<=1}||j(y)||), the composition T j: B->A is bounded with ||T j||_op <= ||T||_op ||j||_op. Derivation directly from the norm definition: for ||y||<=1 in B, ||(T j)(y)|| = ||T(j(y))|| <= ||T||_op ||j(y)|| <= ||T||_op (||j||_op ||y||) <= ||T||_op ||j||_op; take sup over ||y||<=1. (The same sup-of-||w(Z)|| definition, now for a map between the two Banach spaces underlying the two algebras; ||y||<=1 plays the role of ||Z||<=1.) Used in the limit-passage node N1.4 to push limits through the bounded j.
